question_text: Choose the option that best completes the sentence.\n\nSally assisted me in buying my _____ yesterday.
explanation: In English, intensifiers come before ordinal adjectives: “very first” → “very first party dress.”

question_text: Choose the option that best completes the sentence.\n\nYou know that you ____ it before tomorrow.
explanation: “Ought to” expresses obligation appropriate to a deadline: you ought to do it before tomorrow.

question_text: Choose the option that best completes the sentence.\n\nMaryam, the girl in my neighbourhood, treats people as if she _____ better than they are.
explanation: After “as if/as though,” formal usage takes the subjunctive “were.”

question_text: Choose the option that best completes the sentence.\n\nBut for the timely intervention of the police, the thief ______ by the angry mob.
explanation: “But for” + past condition takes the past unreal result: “would have been lynched.”

question_text: Choose the option that best completes the sentence.\n\nMy father loves bringing ___ old memories.
explanation: The phrasal verb is “bring up” (to mention/recall).
